groups.label: Positive clue words
groups.blurb: Usually point to the CORRECT answer.

groups.label: Negative clue words
groups.blurb: Usually point to a WRONG answer.

words.effect_en: Positive when authority help is needed: accident, threat, unpaid fare, traffic control, or a person who cannot safely be left.
words.exception_en: Do not call police for every ordinary customer-service problem; first use calm communication where safe.

words.effect_en: Usually points to the driver checking safety, destination, seat belt use, assisted handover, or customer comfort.
words.exception_en: Not enough by itself if the option asks the driver to do something illegal, unsafe, or beyond the taxi-driver role.

words.meaning_en: ask / inquire / discuss / whether help is needed
words.effect_en: Positive when the driver asks before assisting, confirms wishes, route details, or a problem calmly.
words.exception_en: If immediate emergency action is required, asking too much first may not be the best first step.

words.effect_en: Positive when help is offered respectfully without forcing the passenger.
words.exception_en: Do not assume every passenger needs help; ask or observe first unless the situation is clearly urgent.

words.effect_en: Positive when authorization, work assignment, regular-taxi arrangement, or company process must be confirmed.
words.exception_en: Driver responsibility for immediate safety cannot be handed to employer after the ride has already started.

words.effect_en: Positive when the driver cannot personally decide Kela entitlement, regular-taxi rights, or suitable vehicle arrangement.
words.exception_en: Do not replace official Kela/dispatch process with personal permission from the customer or guardian.

words.effect_en: Strong positive clue when the answer protects the passenger, road users, or the driver.
words.exception_en: Check whether the option is practical and legal; a broad safety word can still be wrong if it ignores a specific rule.

words.effect_en: Positive in school/day-care transport context where the vehicle must have an alcohol interlock.
words.exception_en: Not every taxi requires an alcohol interlock; the school/day-care transport context matters.

words.effect_en: Positive in customer service, complaints, aggression, and passenger comfort situations.
words.exception_en: Politeness cannot override legal/safety duties such as seat belts, safe stopping, or accident response.

words.meaning_en: right destination / sufficient language skill / communicate
words.effect_en: Positive when the driver’s duty is ensuring the customer reaches the correct destination safely.
words.exception_en: Sufficient language skill does not mean fluent conversation or same-language ability.

words.meaning_en: without delay / as soon as possible / immediately
words.effect_en: Usually positive for lost property, emergency response, safety reporting, and urgent assistance.
words.exception_en: Time words are not enough if the action itself is wrong, such as abandoning a passenger quickly.

words.meaning_en: necessary / necessary for carrying out the transport
words.effect_en: Positive for phone/hands-free or communication only when it is genuinely needed for the transport.
words.exception_en: Necessary does not permit speeding, unsafe stopping, or illegal customer requests.

words.meaning_en: hands-free / communication devices / making a call
words.effect_en: Positive only when communication is necessary and safe, especially with hands-free while driving.
words.exception_en: Handheld phone use, messaging, or personal calls during transport are generally negative clues.

words.effect_en: Positive in questions about children, vulnerable customers, freezing weather, care institutions, or agreed assistants.
words.exception_en: Do not expand this into medical/nursing tasks such as undressing or personal care unless specifically required and appropriate.

words.effect_en: Positive for child restraints, seat belts, vehicles, equipment, and safe customer transport.
words.exception_en: Still check the specific object: a suitable taxi or approved restraint is different from any improvised solution.

words.phrase_fi: edullisin tarkoituksenmukainen reitti / tarkoituksenmukainen reitti / edullisin
words.effect_en: Positive when passenger leaves route choice to the driver or asks for route advice.
words.exception_en: Do not blindly choose any route; customer preference and fairness matter.

words.meaning_en: open airways / airways / recovery position / first aid
words.effect_en: Positive in first-aid and seizure/accident questions.
words.exception_en: Do not physically restrain convulsions or move an injured person unless necessary to save life/prevent further danger.

words.meaning_en: guide dog / working dog / identifying harness
words.effect_en: Positive in special-needs questions about guide dogs and working dogs.
words.exception_en: Do not distract, pet, or treat the guide dog as a normal pet during the job.

words.meaning_en: receipt / offering or giving a receipt
words.effect_en: Positive because receipt must be offered/given after the ride/payment.
words.exception_en: Receipt duty is separate from payment disputes; do not take a customer’s card or property by yourself.

words.effect_en: Positive when official traffic instructions override conflicting informal instructions.
words.exception_en: Employer/customer instructions do not override police, traffic controller, or road rules.

words.meaning_en: by frame / four points / brakes on
words.effect_en: Strong positive clue for securing a wheelchair safely in an accessible taxi.
words.exception_en: Wheelchair brakes alone or normal seat belt alone are not enough for wheelchair securing.

words.meaning_en: driving logbook / shift start / break times
words.effect_en: Positive in work-time and driving-logbook duty questions.
words.exception_en: Do not include irrelevant personal details such as meals unless the law/company rule specifically asks.

words.effect_en: Positive in Kela-ride payment questions.
words.exception_en: Only applies when the ride is properly ordered as a Kela ride through the correct process.

words.effect_en: Positive for accessible-taxi pre-departure equipment checks.
words.exception_en: A visual check alone may be too weak if the question asks how to check the lift.

words.effect_en: Positive clue for rear-facing child restraint on the front passenger seat.
words.exception_en: Airbag clue is context-specific; it does not remove normal child-restraint and seat-belt duties.

words.effect_en: Positive in school/day-care transport and general professional behaviour.
words.exception_en: Being friendly or role-model-like still does not mean doing parent/school duties outside the driver role.

words.effect_en: Can be suspicious because it may make an option too absolute.
words.exception_en: Often correct where the rule truly is absolute in that context, such as always using proper alkolukko in school/day-care transport or always offering receipt.

words.effect_en: Often too narrow when it limits a duty too much, such as only front seat, only customer service, only visual check.
words.exception_en: Can be correct in questions about strict legal limits or defined exceptions.

words.effect_en: Often negative when it means without seat belt, restraint, hands-free, payment, checking, or permission.
words.exception_en: Can be neutral as context, for example “without advance booking”; do not judge this word alone.

words.phrase_fi: ei tarvitse / ei tarvi / tarvitse käyttää
words.effect_en: Often wrong when it removes a safety, seat-belt, equipment, or service duty.
words.exception_en: Can be correct when the law truly gives no duty in that exact context, so check who is responsible and the question wording.

words.effect_en: Negative if used to justify speeding, unsafe stopping, or breaking traffic rules.
words.exception_en: Positive answer usually explains the delay, chooses the fastest legal route, and keeps the customer updated.

words.phrase_fi: jos asiakas pyytää / asiakas pyytää / pyytää ajamaan ylinopeutta
words.effect_en: Customer request never permits speeding, unsafe stopping, illegal purchase, or abandoning safety duties.
words.exception_en: Customer request can be valid for normal preferences, route selection, seat choice, or assistance style.

words.phrase_fi: ei koskaan / missään tilanteessa / voi koskaan
words.effect_en: Often too absolute and therefore wrong.
words.exception_en: Can be correct for clearly prohibited behaviour, such as handheld phone use or illegal alcohol-purchase requests.

words.phrase_fi: ilman turvalaitetta / turvalaitetta ei / turvalaite ei
words.effect_en: Usually negative for children and accessibility safety.
words.exception_en: Taxi child-seat exceptions are narrow; under-3 and under-135 contexts need careful rule checking.

words.effect_en: Often suspicious when used to justify bypassing alkolukko, stopping rules, or phone rules.
words.exception_en: Some legal exceptions exist, but they must be explicitly stated and not invented by the driver.

words.effect_en: Negative when safety, child, special-needs, accident, or lost-property duty exists.
words.exception_en: Can be correct only after you have ensured the customer/property/situation is safely handled.

words.effect_en: Usually wrong for seat belt, receipt, and mandatory driver duties.
words.exception_en: Some customer choices are voluntary, but legal safety and service duties are not.

words.meaning_en: speeding / exceed speed limit / speeding fines
words.effect_en: Strong negative clue: hurry or customer promise to pay fines never allows speeding.
words.exception_en: Emergency vehicles/legal emergency rules do not apply to ordinary taxi rides.

words.phrase_fi: asiakas päättää / oikeus valita / saa valita pysähtymispaikan
words.effect_en: Negative when customer choice conflicts with safety, law, route fairness, payment rules, or driver responsibility.
words.exception_en: Customer can choose or influence the route when legal and fair; driver may still need to advise.

words.meaning_en: short / under a minute / under a kilometre
words.effect_en: Usually wrong when it says a short distance/time removes safety or equipment duties.
words.exception_en: Short distance may matter only if the law or question explicitly sets that exception.

words.phrase_fi: takapenkillä ei tarvitse / kuljettajan ei tarvitse käyttää turvavyötä / ei tarvitse käyttää turvavyötä
words.meaning_en: back-seat/driver does not need seat belt
words.effect_en: Usually negative: driver and passengers must follow seat-belt rules.
words.exception_en: Responsibility for a 15+ passenger may be the passenger’s, but that does not make seat-belt use optional.

words.phrase_fi: henkilökohtaisia puheluita / lähettää viestejä / katsella televisiokuvaa
words.meaning_en: personal calls / send messages / watch screen
words.effect_en: Negative while driving or with customer on board.
words.exception_en: Work-related communication may be allowed only if necessary and handled safely.

words.phrase_fi: käsipuhelin / puhelin kädessä / pitää puhelinta kädessä
words.meaning_en: handheld phone / phone in hand / without hands-free
words.effect_en: Negative while driving; communication should be hands-free and only when necessary.
words.exception_en: If stopped safely and legally, communication may be handled differently.

words.meaning_en: by force / physically / shout / push / hold tightly
words.effect_en: Negative in aggression, assistance, disability, and seizure situations.
words.exception_en: Physical action is only for immediate safety/first aid and must be proportionate; do not restrain convulsions.

words.phrase_fi: ei alkolukkoa / ilman alkolukkoa / olla käyttämättä alkolukkoa
words.meaning_en: no alcohol interlock / without interlock / switch vehicle
words.effect_en: Negative in school/day-care transport where an alcohol interlock is required.
words.exception_en: Not every taxi needs alkolukko, so this clue depends on the transport type.

words.phrase_fi: huoltajan lupa / huoltajan valtuutus / kirjallinen lupa
words.effect_en: Suspicious when used to override child seat-belt duties or official Kela/regular-taxi processes.
words.exception_en: Guardian involvement can be relevant for a child travelling alone, but it does not cancel legal safety duties.

words.effect_en: Often negative when it is used to justify buying alcohol or bypassing a formal process.
words.exception_en: A written authorisation may matter in other legal contexts, but not for illegal or unsafe requests.

words.phrase_fi: odottaa autossa / odotan autossa / ei tarvitse poistua
words.meaning_en: wait in car / no need to leave car
words.effect_en: Often wrong in assistance, slow passenger, visually impaired, wheelchair, or safe-exit questions.
words.exception_en: May be acceptable when no help is needed and leaving the vehicle would create a safety problem.

words.effect_en: Negative in passenger-lift or equipment check questions.
words.exception_en: A visual check can be part of inspection, but not enough if functional testing is required.

words.phrase_fi: vain asiakaspalveluun / keskittyä vain asiakaspalveluun / vain omaan ajoneuvoon
words.meaning_en: only customer service / focus only on one thing
words.effect_en: Wrong when it ignores traffic safety, anticipation, or special passenger needs.
words.exception_en: Customer service is important but never replaces legal and safety duties.

words.phrase_fi: ei kuljettajan vastuulla / vastuu päättyy / ei ole sinun vastuullasi
words.meaning_en: not driver responsibility / responsibility ends / customer responsible
words.effect_en: Often negative when it tries to remove the driver’s duty to assist or ensure safety.
words.exception_en: Can be correct for 15+ seat-belt responsibility, but driver still manages safe transport.

words.phrase_fi: oman harkinnan mukaan / minkä hyvänsä / minkä hyvänsä
words.meaning_en: at own discretion / any route / anything
words.effect_en: Often wrong when it ignores rules, passenger interest, route fairness, or legal limits.
words.exception_en: Driver judgement matters only within legal and professional boundaries.

words.effect_en: Strong negative clue: driver should not take payment card, wallet, or valuables by force.
words.exception_en: Use proper payment handling, discussion, dispatch/company process, or police if necessary.

words.effect_en: Negative in visually impaired/guide-dog service questions.
words.exception_en: Guide dog may ride with the customer, but should not be distracted.

words.phrase_fi: vaatii avustajan / ei mukana avustaja / avustaja ei ole mukana
words.effect_en: Negative when it assumes disability means refusing service or requiring an assistant.
words.exception_en: If an agreed receiver/assistant is missing for a vulnerable person, wait or arrange safe handover.
